translated: I'll talk more about this while we watch the story later, but regarding Ena in this event,
and what I've been keeping in mind when singing Niigo's recent songs as Ena...
As her voice actress, I've personally made a conscious effort to show that she's never going back to her old self. Even if the pace is slow,
Ena is moving forward bit by bit. Because of the nature of Niigo's music, the lyrics and atmosphere
tend to lean toward being quite negative or gloomy at times.
I try not to let myself get pulled too far into that. Honestly, it might be easier to include
nuances of tears or negativity to make a line or a song really grab the listeners' attention,
or to make them think, "Oh, I love this phrase."
I know those are the things that really stick in people's ears.
But if I did that, it would just be my own desire for validation as a performer.
I want to make sure Ena's songs and lines convey that she's looking forward, even if it's just a little.
So even with the same words, I try to make sure you can feel her progress.
I've been gradually increasing that sense of positivity based on the story.
That's what I focused on while performing her this time.
And,
not just this time, but in recent songs too, I think Ena has realized that the world is warmer
and people are more willing to help than she originally thought.
I kept that in mind during the recording.
[CORNER: Fan Letter Corner] Moving on,
I'd like to read another letter.
This one is from Sudachi Udon-san.
"Good evening, Minori-san."
Good evening!
"I read the Colors of Pure Sense story."
Thank you so much.
"What left an impression on me was the scene in Episode 7 where Ena gets so excited because she's having so much fun drawing."
"Ena used to only get self-validation from others, like through her selfies, but she finally realized she's fine just as she is."
"She realized her world and expression can be as free as she wants. Her world gradually became more colorful,"
"and the way her eyes sparkled as she enjoyed drawing really came through in your voice, Minori-san."
"It actually brought me to tears."
"I want to keep watching over and supporting Ena as she resolves to never give up on her art and her dreams."
"Minori-san, thank you for showing us the colors of Ena's heart so vividly through your voice." What a lovely message.
Thank you so much for those kind words.
I really appreciate it.
No, thank YOU for receiving it that way.
I always hope that Ena's heart and feelings reach all of you.
Of course, she's a game character and a 2D creation,
but Ena is truly alive to me.
Nobody knows what her future holds yet,
and that's why...
I want to deliver something that feels real and raw, so I'm happy to hear your thoughts.
Thank you.
Alright.
I have so much more I want to talk about while we watch the story, and if I'm not careful,
five minutes will pass in the blink of an eye.
So let's keep things moving along!
